title: Efficiency and perfect competition
description: Practice what you have learned about productive efficiency and allocative efficiency in perfect competition in the short run and the long run.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of following best describes a perfectly competitive firm in the short run and the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Short run: allocatively efficient; long run: allocatively efficient and productively efficient
Short run: neither allocatively efficient nor productively efficient; long run: allocatively efficient and productively efficient
Short run: productively efficient only; long run: allocatively efficient only
Short run: allocatively efficient and productively efficient; long run: neither allocatively efficient nor productively efficient
Short run: neither allocatively efficient nor productively efficient; long run: neither allocatively efficient nor productively efficient

assessment_items.question_content:
**If a firm operates in a perfectly competitive market, what must be true about the firm's efficiency in the short run and the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Allocatively efficient in the short run; both allocatively efficient and productively efficient in the long run
Neither allocatively efficient nor productively efficient in the short run; Both allocatively efficient and productively efficient in the long run
Productively efficient in the short run; allocatively efficient but not productively efficient in the long run
Allocatively efficient and productively efficient in the short run; neither allocatively efficient nor productively efficient in the long run
Neither allocatively efficient nor productively efficient in the short run;  neither allocatively efficient nor productively efficient in the long run

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true about a perfectly competitive firm in the long run?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It produces the quantity where marginal benefit equals marginal cost and the average total cost is minimized.
It produces the quantity where marginal benefit equals marginal cost and marginal cost is greater than the average total cost.
It produces the quantity where marginal benefit exceeds marginal cost and marginal cost equals average total cost.
It produces the quantity where marginal benefit exceeds the marginal cost and the average total cost is minimized.
It produces a quantity that is allocatively efficient but not productively efficient.

assessment_items.question_content:
Meliah's Maps produces road maps in a perfectly competitive market. 

**What is true about the quantity of maps this firm produces in the long run?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Marginal benefit equals marginal cost and the average total cost is minimized.
Marginal benefit equals marginal cost and marginal cost is greater than the average total cost.
Marginal benefit exceeds marginal cost and marginal cost equals average total cost.
Marginal benefit exceeds the marginal cost and the average total cost is minimized.
The quantity is allocatively efficient but not productively efficient.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true if typical firms in a perfectly competitive market are allocatively efficient and produce at the lowest possible cost per unit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The market is in long-run equilibrium.
Firms are earning economic profits and more firms will enter this industry.
Firms are making economic losses and more firms will exit this industry.
Firms will decrease their average total costs if they increase output
The market supply will shift right

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is most likely if all firms in a perfectly competitive market produce a quantity that is both allocatively and productively efficient?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The market is in long-run equilibrium.
Firms are earning economic profits and more firms will enter this industry.
Firms are making economic losses and more firms will exit this industry.
Firms will decrease their average total costs if they increase output
The market supply will shift right

assessment_items.question_content:
The coffee industry is perfectly competitive. All firms in the coffee industry are producing an allocatively efficient quantity and are productively efficient.

**Which of the following is most likely true based on this information?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The market is in long-run equilibrium.
Firms are earning economic profits and more firms will enter this industry.
Firms are making economic losses and more firms will exit this industry.
Firms will decrease their average total costs if they increase output
The market supply will shift right

assessment_items.question_content:
**If the market for donuts is perfectly competitive and all firms are producing a quantity that generates no deadweight loss and the cost per unit is minimized, which of the following is most likely true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The market is in long-run equilibrium.
Firms are earning economic profits and more firms will enter this industry.
Firms are making economic losses and more firms will exit this industry.
Firms will decrease their average total costs if they increase output
The market supply will shift right
